crie.title: Administration
crie.reconciliation: R-02 (Administration = WF-005; WF-004 is Output Generation)
crie.boundary: R-17: WF-005 produces/refreshes DASHBOARD DATA only. It renders NO UI. Dashboards are the admin.* views surfaced by the external BI tool.
crie.slice: PR-1 slice: only nodes whose backends already exist in migrations 0001-0029 are implemented (Repository Health, Repository Analytics, Cost Report, Orphan Sweep, threshold evaluation, alert persistence). Unbuilt sub-workflows are intentionally omitted (see deferred_dependencies) rather than stubbed with dead executeWorkflow calls.
crie.aggregation: Each upstream branch tags its output with a stable _source identifier (repository_health / repository_statistics / cost_report / orphan_sweep). The Evaluate Alert Thresholds Code node aggregates by _source (never by payload shape), which is deterministic and resilient to schema changes. No Merge node is used (this repo has none; the Code-node fan-in is the established pattern).
crie.failure_isolation: Only the Orphan Sweep node is failure-isolated (onError: continueRegularOutput) so a sweep failure does not block operational reporting. A reporting-node (Postgres) failure aborts the run by design — it signals a genuine DB fault.
crie.rollout: Staged over two PRs. PR-1 (this) builds the slice and wires the Orphan Sweep by CALLING SW-016 — so WF-005 becomes a second sweep scheduler alongside SW-016's own Schedule Trigger. PR-2 (small follow-up) disables SW-016's Schedule Trigger to reach a single scheduler. The interim dual-scheduler period is intentional; the sweep is append-only/idempotent so no double remediation occurs; temporary duplicate alerts are an accepted trade-off during the staged rollout.
crie.binding: The Orphan Sweep node calls SW-016 via executeWorkflow. Imports mint new IDs — RE-BIND this node to SW-016 by ID after import (CLAUDE.md rule 4). waitForSubWorkflow is true so WF-005 receives SW-016's summary before aggregating.
crie.ci: WF-005 is deliberately NOT added to ci/active_workflows.txt in PR-1; it is added in PR-2 when it becomes the production scheduler. It can be validated ad-hoc by passing its path explicitly to ci/validate_workflows.py.

deferred_dependencies.note: Intentionally omitted from the PR-1 slice — backends not yet built. NOT stubbed with dead executeWorkflow calls (the CI validator would flag those). Add when implemented.

items.impact: feeds monitoring.health_checks; Repository Health is sparse until built
no per-run Workflow/AI/Audit log for the admin run
no telemetry span for the admin run
alerts are persisted but not pushed (Email/Slack/Teams)

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: §157 WF-005 runs on schedule. Cron is config-driven ($vars.CRIE_ADMIN_SCHEDULE_CRON); NO hardcoded cadence (Principle 7).

nodes.name: Initialize
nodes.notes: §159 Standard Workflow Pattern: Trigger -> Initialize -> fan-out. Correlation ID ties every downstream record (alerts) to this run.

nodes.name: Repository Health
nodes.notes: §528 Repository Health. repository.repository_health() (migration 0015) returns TABLE(component,state,checked_at) from monitoring.health_checks. Data is sparse until SW-019 (deferred) populates health_checks. Tagged _source='repository_health' for source-keyed aggregation.

nodes.name: Repository Analytics
nodes.notes: §533/§560 Repository Analytics. repository.repository_statistics() (migration 0015) returns document/KU/evidence/chunk/embedding counts. Tagged _source='repository_statistics'.

nodes.name: Cost Report
nodes.notes: §198/§564 Cost Report. admin.vw_cost_intelligence (migration 0024) aggregates monitoring.ai_requests. Sparse until ingestion AI-cost logging populates ai_requests. Tagged _source='cost_report'.

nodes.name: Orphan Sweep (SW-016)
nodes.notes: Folds in the Orphan Sweep by calling SW-016 (via its passthrough 'When Called by WF-005' trigger). waitForSubWorkflow=true so WF-005 receives SW-016's run summary before aggregating. onError='continueRegularOutput' so a sweep failure does NOT block operational reporting. REBIND to SW-016 by ID after import (CLAUDE.md rule 4). If the n8n instance predates the node-level 'onError' property, use 'continueOnFail': true instead.

nodes.name: Tag Orphan Sweep
nodes.notes: Tags SW-016's summary item with _source='orphan_sweep' (executeWorkflow output cannot self-tag in SQL). includeOtherFields=true preserves SW-016's summary fields (remediated_count, etc.).

nodes.name: Evaluate Alert Thresholds
nodes.notes: §387/§569 Alert evaluation + source-keyed aggregator. Reads all four branches via $input.all() and groups by _source. Defensive: tolerates any absent branch without throwing. Emits zero items when nothing breaches (Persist Alerts then simply does not run).

nodes.name: Persist Alerts

parameters.columns: alert_type, severity, source, message, context, correlation_id

nodes.notes: Writes to monitoring.alerts (migration 0026). context is a JSON string cast into the jsonb column. admin.vw_alert_center surfaces these in the BI tool (§569, R-17).
